name: Percentages — Out of a Hundred
whatIsIt: PER CENT means 'per hundred'. 25% means 25 out of every 100, which is the fraction 25/100 = 1/4 = the decimal 0.25. Percentages are a common language for parts: 50% is half, 100% is all, 10% is one-tenth. To find a percentage OF an amount, turn the % into a fraction or decimal and multiply.
whyNeeded: Percentages run daily life: exam marks, '20% off' sales, battery level, phone storage, interest at the bank, cricket strike rates. One shared 'out of 100' language lets us compare fairly and spot a good (or bad) deal.
realLifeUses: A '30% off' tag in a sale
Scoring 18 out of 20 in a test (= 90%)
A 5% service charge on a restaurant bill

vocabulary.meaning: An amount taken off the price, often given as a percentage.
In percentage sums, 'of' means multiply (10% of 200).

story.title: The Sale Sticker
story.text: Farhan eyes a cricket bat priced ₹800 with a '25% OFF' sticker. 'How much do I save?' he asks. The shopkeeper says, 'Work out 25% of 800.' Farhan knows 25% is one quarter, and a quarter of 800 is 200. 'I save ₹200, so I pay ₹600!' The shopkeeper nods — 'percentages tell you the deal.'
story.extractedProblem: A ₹800 bat has 25% off. How much is saved, and what is the final price?

visual.caption: 25% = 25/100 = 1/4 = 0.25. Percent is just another name for a part of the whole.

teachingGallery.title: Same value, three names
teachingGallery.note: Percent ↔ fraction ↔ decimal are the same number.

standardMethod.summary: Percent means out of 100. Convert to a fraction (÷100) or decimal, and remember 'of' means multiply.
steps: Read the percent as 'that many out of 100' (e.g. 20% = 20/100 = 0.2).
To find a percent OF an amount: multiply the amount by the fraction/decimal.
For a discount: find the % off, then subtract it from the price.

workedExamples.problem: A ₹1200 phone has a 10% discount. What is the final price?

commonMistakes.mistake: Treating a percent as a whole number (using 25 instead of 25/100).
commonMistakes.fix: Percent means OUT OF 100. Convert first: 25% = 25/100 = 0.25 before you multiply.

commonMistakes.mistake: Giving the amount saved as the final price you pay.
commonMistakes.fix: The discount is what you SAVE. Subtract it from the original to get the price you PAY.

easy.q: 50% of an amount is the same as…

medium.q: A ₹500 bag has 20% off. How many rupees are saved?

challenge.q: A ₹1200 cycle has a 25% discount. What is the final price (in ₹)?
A ₹400 shirt is '10% off'. Meena says 'so it costs ₹40'. What's wrong?

options.label: ₹40 is only the saving — the price is 400 − 40 = ₹360

challenge.answer: ₹40 is only the saving — the price is 400 − 40 = ₹360

questions.q: A ₹250 book has 20% off. Final price (in ₹)?
25% is the same as which fraction?

teachBackPrompt: Find a real sale tag or your latest test marks. Show a grown-up how to turn it into a percentage (or a percentage into a saving), and explain why 25% is the same as one quarter and 0.25.

revisionCard.summary: Percent = out of 100. 25% = 25/100 = 1/4 = 0.25. To find a percent OF an amount, multiply (10% = ÷10, 25% = ÷4, 50% = ÷2). Discount = amount SAVED; subtract it from the price to get what you PAY.
